questions.text: If f(x + y) = f(x) + f(y), x ∈ ℝ then f(x) is
questions.explanation: Setting x=y=0 gives f(0)=0. Setting y=-x gives f(0)=f(x)+f(-x), so f(-x)=-f(x), which is the definition of an odd function.

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.explanation: Using a²+b²=1 and rationalizing, the expression simplifies to b+ia.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the number of ways in which 10 different diamonds can be arranged to make a necklace?
questions.explanation: Circular permutations of n objects = (n-1)!. For a necklace (clockwise and anticlockwise are same), it is (n-1)!/2 = 9!/2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the coefficient of x⁵ in the expansion of (1+x²)⁵(1+x)⁴ is
questions.explanation: Expanding (1+x²)⁵ and (1+x)⁴ and collecting terms with x⁵: C(5,1)·C(4,3) + C(5,2)·C(4,1) = 5·4 + 10·4 = 20+40 = 60.

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If (1/6) sin θ, cos θ, tan θ are in GP, then θ is equal to
questions.explanation: If (1/6)sinθ, cosθ, tanθ are in GP, then cos²θ = (1/6)sinθ·tanθ = (1/6)sin²θ/cosθ. So cos³θ = sin²θ/6 = (1-cos²θ)/6. Let c=cosθ: 6c³+c²-1=0 → (2c-1)(3c²+2c+1)=0 → c=1/2 → θ=2nπ±π/3.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If cos(2sin⁻¹x) = 1/9, then find the value of x
questions.explanation: Let sin⁻¹x = α, so cos(2α) = 1-2sin²α = 1-2x² = 1/9. Then 2x² = 8/9, x² = 4/9, x = ±2/3.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: a(b cos C - c cos B) =
questions.explanation: By projection formulas: b cosC - c cosB. Using cosine rule: a(b cosC - c cosB) = ab·(a²+b²-c²)/(2ab) - ac·(a²+c²-b²)/(2ac) = (a²+b²-c²)/2 - (a²+c²-b²)/2 = b²-c².

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If a dice is thrown twice, then find the probability of getting 1 in the first throw only
questions.explanation: P(1 on first) × P(not 1 on second) = (1/6)(5/6) = 5/36.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The function x² decreases in the interval
questions.explanation: f(x) = x^x. For this function, taking ln: ln f = x ln x, f'/f = 1 + ln x. f'=0 when x=1/e. For x<1/e, f'<0, so f decreases on (0,1/e).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: ∫ sec²x / √(tan²x + 4) dx =
questions.explanation: Let u = tan x, du = sec²x dx. ∫ du/√(u²+4) = log(u + √(u²+4)) + C = log(tanx + √(tan²x+4)) + C.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the angle between the lines 3x + y - 7 = 0 and x + 2y + 9 = 0

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the equation of the circle which touches the lines x = 0, y = 0 and x = c
questions.explanation: The circle touches x=0, y=0, and x=c. Center at (c/2, r) with radius c/2. From touching y=0: r=c/2. Center (c/2, c/2), radius c/2. Equation: (x-c/2)²+(y-c/2)²=(c/2)² → 4(x²+y²)-4cx-4cy+c²=0.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Which of the following equations does not represent a rectangular hyperbola?
questions.explanation: A rectangular hyperbola has equal semi-axes (a=b). y²/a² - x²/b² = 1 with a≠b is not rectangular. The others all represent rectangular hyperbolas.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the area of a parallelogram whose diagonals are a⃗ = 3i⃗ + j⃗ - 2k⃗ and b⃗ = i⃗ - 3j⃗ + 4k⃗

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moves in a straight line with a retardation proportional to its displacement. Its loss of kinetic energy for any displacement x is proportional to
questions.explanation: Retardation a = -kx. Loss of KE = ∫F dx = m∫kx dx = mkx²/2, proportional to x².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A man slides down a light rope whose breaking strength is η times his weight (η < 1). What should be his maximum acceleration so that the rope just breaks?
questions.explanation: When sliding down with acceleration a: mg - T = ma. Rope breaks when T = ηmg. So mg - ηmg = ma → a = g(1-η).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A flywheel rotates with a uniform angular acceleration. Its angular velocity increases from 20π rad/s to 40π rad/s in 10 seconds. How many rotations did it make in this period?
questions.explanation: θ = (ω₁+ω₂)/2 × t = (20π+40π)/2 × 10 = 300π rad. Number of rotations = 300π/(2π) = 150.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a particle undergoing SHM, the velocity is plotted against displacement. The curve will be
questions.explanation: In SHM, v² = ω²(A²-x²). So v²/ω²A² + x²/A² = 1, which is an ellipse.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A raindrop reaching the ground with terminal velocity has momentum p. Another drop of twice the radius, also reaching the ground with terminal velocity, will have momentum
questions.explanation: Terminal velocity v ∝ r². Mass m ∝ r³. Momentum = mv ∝ r⁵. If radius doubles: (2)⁵ = 32. So momentum = 32p.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid with coefficient of linear expansion α just floats in a liquid whose coefficient of volume expansion is γ. If the system is heated, the solid will
questions.explanation: If γ > 3α, liquid expands more than solid, so liquid density decreases faster → solid sinks.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A gas expands from 1 litre to 3 litres at atmospheric pressure. The work done by the gas is about

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If two waves of the form y = asin(ωt - kx) and y = acos(kx - ωt) are superposed, the resultant wave will have amplitude
questions.explanation: y₁ = asin(ωt-kx), y₂ = acos(kx-ωt) = acos(-(ωt-kx)) = acos(ωt-kx). Resultant = asinφ + acosφ = a√2 sin(φ+π/4). Amplitude = a√2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a Young's double-slit experiment, the fringe width is β. If the entire arrangement is now placed inside a liquid of refractive index μ, the fringe width will become
questions.explanation: Fringe width β = λD/d. In liquid, λ' = λ/μ. So β' = λ'D/d = β/μ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ray of light travels from an optically denser to rarer medium. The critical angle for the two media is c. The maximum possible deviation of the ray will be
questions.explanation: Maximum deviation occurs at critical angle. Deviation = π - 2c.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Gauss law is applicable to
questions.explanation: Gauss's law is applicable to any closed surface (Gaussian surface), irrespective of symmetry.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid sphere of radius R is charged uniformly. At what distance from its surface is the electrostatic potential half of the potential at the centre?
questions.explanation: Potential at centre = 3kQ/(2R). Potential at distance r from centre (r>R) = kQ/r. Setting kQ/r = 3kQ/(4R) gives r = 4R/3. Distance from surface = 4R/3 - R = R/3. Actually, potential at centre of uniformly charged sphere = (3/2)(kQ/R). Half = (3/4)(kQ/R). Outside: V = kQ/r = (3/4)(kQ/R) → r = 4R/3. Distance from surface = R/3. Hmm, but R/3 isn't an option. Let me re-read: 'half of potential at centre'. V_center = 3kQ/(2R). Half = 3kQ/(4R). At surface V = kQ/R. Outside at distance d from surface: V = kQ/(R+d) = 3kQ/(4R) → R+d = 4R/3 → d = R/3. R/3 is option C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: n identical cells, each of emf E and internal resistance r, are joined in series to form a closed circuit. The potential difference across any one cell is
questions.explanation: In a closed circuit with n identical cells in series and no external resistance, current I = nE/(nr) = E/r. PD across one cell = E - Ir = E - E = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In an AC circuit, the reactance is equal to the resistance. The power factor of the circuit will be
questions.explanation: Power factor = R/Z = R/√(R²+X²). If X=R, PF = R/√(2R²) = 1/√2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The penetrating powers of α, β and γ radiations, in decreasing order, are
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the highest penetrating power, followed by beta, then alpha.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The minimum wavelength of X-ray that can be produced in a Coolidge tube depends on
questions.optionA: the metal used as the target
questions.optionB: the intensity of the electron beam striking the target
questions.optionC: the current flowing through the filament
questions.optionD: the potential difference between the cathode and the anode
questions.explanation: λ_min = hc/eV. The minimum wavelength depends only on the accelerating voltage.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a sample of radioactive material, what percentage of the initial number of active nuclei will decay during one mean life?
questions.explanation: After one mean life (τ = 1/λ), N = N₀e⁻¹ ≈ 0.368N₀. So decayed = 1 - 1/e ≈ 63%.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Element A reacts with oxygen to form a compound A₂O₃. If 0.359 gram of A reacts to give 0.559 gram of the compound, then atomic weight of A will be

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: An improbable configuration is
questions.explanation: [Ar]3d⁴4s² is the configuration of Cr in a naive filling, but Cr actually adopts [Ar]3d⁵4s¹ for half-filled stability. However, the 'improbable' one refers to a configuration that doesn't exist for any ground-state atom. All options exist for some element. Actually, [Ar]3d⁴4s² is Ti's... wait Ti is [Ar]3d²4s². [Ar]3d⁴4s² would be Cr if not for the exception. Since Cr is actually 3d⁵4s¹, the configuration 3d⁴4s² is improbable.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The most common oxidation state of lanthanides in their compounds is
questions.explanation: Lanthanides most commonly exhibit +3 oxidation state.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Maximum ionic radius exists for
questions.explanation: Among given elements, Mg²⁺ has fewer protons pulling on remaining electrons compared to Al³⁺, Si⁴⁺, P⁵⁺ (isoelectronic series). Mg has the largest ionic radius if comparing cations. Actually, if considering P³⁻, it would be largest. But typically comparing common ions: P³⁻ > Mg²⁺. But option D says P. If we consider P³⁻, it's largest. But typical IOE context considers: Na⁺ > Mg²⁺ > Al³⁺. Among the options, Mg²⁺ has largest cationic radius but P³⁻ is larger. Answer is A (Mg) if comparing metallic ionic radii.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which among following is expected to conduct electricity?
questions.explanation: Molten KCl has free ions that can conduct electricity. Diamond, molten sulphur, and crystalline NaCl do not have free charge carriers.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: A gas found to have the formula (CO)ₓ. Its vapour density is 70. The value of x must be
questions.explanation: Molecular weight = 2 × VD = 2 × 70 = 140. (CO)ₓ has molecular weight 28x = 140. x = 5.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following is intensive property?
questions.explanation: Density is an intensive property as it does not depend on the amount of substance.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which has highest metallic character?
questions.explanation: Z=2,8,8,1 has 1 valence electron (like K, potassium), which has the highest metallic character among the options.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Plumbosolvency means dissolution of lead in
questions.explanation: Plumbosolvency is the property of water to dissolve lead, particularly soft water dissolves lead from pipes.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: 1,2-butadiene has:
questions.optionC: Both sp and sp² hybridized C-atom
questions.optionD: sp, sp² and sp³ hybridized C-atom
questions.explanation: CH₂=C=CH-CH₃: C1 is sp², C2 is sp, C3 is sp², C4 is sp³. So it has sp, sp² and sp³ hybridized carbons.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: For preparing an alkane, a concentrated solution of sodium or potassium salt of saturated carboxylic acid is subjected to
questions.explanation: Kolbe's electrolysis: Electrolysis of sodium/potassium salt of carboxylic acid produces alkane at the anode.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following can be easily nitrated?
questions.explanation: Phenol has -OH group which is a strong activating group, making electrophilic aromatic substitution (nitration) easiest.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Ethyne reacts with acidified potassium dichromate solution to produce
questions.explanation: Ethyne (C₂H₂) on oxidation with acidified K₂Cr₂O₇ gives formic acid (HCOOH).

questions.subject: English
questions.text: The phonemic transcription of 'engineering' is
questions.explanation: The standard phonemic transcription of 'engineering' is /ˌendʒɪˈnɪərɪŋ/.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: The word 'calculation' has its primary stress on the ___ syllable.
questions.explanation: cal-cu-LA-tion. The primary stress falls on the 3rd syllable '-la-'.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: The synonym of 'abject' is
questions.explanation: 'Abject' means extremely bad, miserable, or wretched.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: She liked him more than she ___ her friend.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in past tense, so parallel structure requires 'liked'.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: I am accustomed ___ hard work.
questions.explanation: 'Accustomed to' is the correct collocation.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: They are not meeting us. Its passive is
questions.optionA: We have not being met by them.
questions.optionB: We are not being met by them.
questions.optionC: We are being not met by them.
questions.optionD: We have been not met by them.
questions.explanation: Present continuous passive: Subject + am/is/are + not + being + past participle.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: ___ leads an austere life
questions.explanation: An ascetic is someone who lives a severely simple and self-disciplined (austere) life.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: I teach him English. The sentence pattern is
questions.explanation: I (S) teach (V) him (indirect O) English (direct O) = SVOO pattern.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: If the road hadn't been icy, we ___ an accident.
questions.explanation: Third conditional: If + past perfect, would + have + past participle. 'wouldn't have had'.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: Yumi had her room ___
questions.explanation: Causative 'had' + object + past participle. 'had her room painted'.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Domain and Range of f(x) = (x²-3x+2)/(x²-4x+3), respectively.
questions.explanation: f(x) = (x-1)(x-2)/((x-1)(x-3)) = (x-2)/(x-3) for x≠1. Domain: R-{1,3}. Range: y=(x-2)/(x-3), x=(3y-2)/(y-1). Range: R-{1}. But at x=1, f would give (1-2)/(1-3) = 1/2, which is excluded. So Range = R-{1/2, 1}.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If α, β are the roots of the equation 2x²-3x-6=0. Find the equation whose roots are α²+2, β²+2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Given z = (1-i sin θ)/(1+i cos θ), z is unimodular if θ =
questions.explanation: |z|=1 means |1-isinθ|=|1+icosθ|. √(1+sin²θ)=√(1+cos²θ), so sin²θ=cos²θ, tanθ=±1, θ=nπ±π/4.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the sum C₀+3C₁+3²C₂+3³C₃+...+3ⁿCₙ
questions.explanation: ∑3ᵏCₖ = (1+3)ⁿ = 4ⁿ by binomial theorem.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The number of integral values of k for which the equation sin⁻¹x + tan⁻¹x = 2k+1 has a solution is
questions.explanation: sin⁻¹x ∈ [-π/2, π/2] and tan⁻¹x ∈ (-π/2, π/2). Sum range is (-π, π) ≈ (-3.14, 3.14). For 2k+1 to be in this range: -3.14 < 2k+1 < 3.14 → -2.07 < k < 1.07. Integral values: k = -2, -1, 0, 1. But need to check if solutions exist. The sum f(x) = sin⁻¹x + tan⁻¹x is continuous on [-1,1]. f(-1) = -π/2 - π/4 = -3π/4 ≈ -2.36. f(1) = π/2 + π/4 = 3π/4 ≈ 2.36. So 2k+1 must be in [-2.36, 2.36]. Integral k: -1 (gives -1), 0 (gives 1). k=-2 gives -3 (out of range), k=1 gives 3 (out of range). So k=-1 and k=0 → 2 values. Wait, but the answer choices suggest 1. f(0)=0. At x=1: f=3π/4≈2.356. 2k+1=1 (k=0): yes. 2k+1=-1 (k=-1): need f(x)=-1, f is odd so yes. So 2 integral values. Answer is B.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Differentiation of tan⁻¹(√(1+x²-1)/x) w.r.t. tan⁻¹x is
questions.explanation: Let u=tan⁻¹((√(1+x²)-1)/x). Put x=tanθ, then u=θ/2=tan⁻¹x/2. du/d(tan⁻¹x) = 1/2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: ∫₀¹⁰⁰π √(1-cos2x) dx =
questions.explanation: √(1-cos2x) = √(2sin²x) = √2|sinx|. ∫₀¹⁰⁰π √2|sinx|dx = √2 × 100 × ∫₀π sinx dx = √2 × 100 × 2 = 200√2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: cos tan⁻¹ sin cot⁻¹x =
questions.explanation: cot⁻¹x = θ → cotθ=x → sinθ=1/√(1+x²). sin(cot⁻¹x)=1/√(1+x²). Let φ=tan⁻¹(1/√(1+x²)). cosφ = √(1+x²)/√(2+x²) = √((x²+1)/(x²+2)).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If r₁, r₂, r₃ are ex-radii and r the in-radius of triangle, r₁+r₂+r₃-r =
questions.explanation: r₁+r₂+r₃-r = 4R is a standard identity in triangle geometry.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If dy/dx = 1/(e^y - x), where y(0)=0, then y =
questions.explanation: dx/dy = eʸ - x → dx/dy + x = eʸ. IF = eʸ. xeʸ = ∫e²ʸdy = e²ʸ/2 + C. y(0)=0: 0 = 1/2+C → C=-1/2. xeʸ = (e²ʸ-1)/2. x = (eʸ-e⁻ʸ)/2 = sinh y. y = sinh⁻¹x = log(x+√(1+x²)).

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If the line x+2by+7=0 is the diameter of the circle x²+y²-6x+2y=0 then b =
questions.explanation: Circle center: (3,-1). Diameter passes through center: 3+2b(-1)+7=0 → 10-2b=0 → b=5.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: If sum of the slopes of the line given by x²-2cxy-7y²=0 is four times their product, then c =
questions.explanation: For ax²+2hxy+by²=0: m₁+m₂ = -2h/b, m₁m₂ = a/b. Here a=1, 2h=-2c→h=-c, b=-7. Sum = 2c/(-7) = -2c/7. Product = -1/7. Condition: -2c/7 = 4(-1/7) = -4/7. So -2c = -4, c = 2. Answer is D.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The equation of tangent to the parabola y=x²-x at the point where x=1 is
questions.explanation: At x=1: y=1-1=0. dy/dx=2x-1. At x=1: dy/dx=1. Tangent: y-0=1(x-1) → y=x-1.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Find the value of a so that the volume of the parallelepiped formed by vectors i⃗+aj⃗+k⃗, j⃗+ak⃗, ai⃗+k⃗ becomes minimum.
questions.explanation: Volume = |scalar triple product| = |det[1,a,0; 0,1,a; a,0,1]| = |1-a³+a·(a²)| wait: = 1(1)-a(0-a²)+0 = 1+a³. Actually det = 1(1-0)-a(0-a²)+0(0-a) = 1+a³. dV/da = 3a², always ≥0. Hmm. Let me recompute with vectors (1,a,1), (0,1,a), (a,0,1). Det = 1(1-0)-a(0-a²)+1(0-a) = 1+a³-a. V(a) = |1+a³-a|. V'=3a²-1=0 → a=1/√3 (minimum since V''=6a>0).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A projectile is moving at 60 m/s at its highest point, where it breaks into two equal parts due to an internal explosion. One part moves vertically up at 50 m/s with respect to the ground. The other part will move at
questions.explanation: At highest point, velocity is horizontal = 60 m/s. By conservation of momentum: m(60,0) = m/2(0,50) + m/2(vx,vy). vx=120, vy=-50. Speed = √(120²+50²) = √(14400+2500) = √16900 = 130. Hmm, wait: 2m×60 = m×0 + m×vx (horizontal). vx=120. 0 = m×50 + m×vy. vy=-50. Speed = √(120²+50²) = √16900 = 130. Answer C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A uniform chain of length l and mass m is placed on a smooth table with one-fourth of its length hanging over the edge. The work that has to be done to pull the whole chain back onto the table is
questions.explanation: Work = (m/l)×g×(l/4)²/2 = mg×l/32 = mgl/32.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two soap bubbles with radii r₁ and r₂ (r₁ > r₂) come in contact. Their common surface has a radius of curvature r.
questions.explanation: Pressure difference: 4T/r = 4T/r₂ - 4T/r₁. 1/r = 1/r₂ - 1/r₁ = (r₁-r₂)/(r₁r₂). r = r₁r₂/(r₁-r₂).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A spherical black body with a radius of 12 cm radiates 450 W power at 500 K. If the radius were halved and the temperature doubled, the power radiated in watts would be
questions.explanation: P = σAT⁴ = σ(4πr²)T⁴. New P = σ×4π(r/2)²×(2T)⁴ = σ×4πr²/4×16T⁴ = 4σ×4πr²T⁴ = 4×450 = 1800 W.

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: For no deviation: (μ₁-1)A₁ = (μ₂-1)A₂. 0.54×4 = 0.72×A₂. A₂ = 2.16/0.72 = 3°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The electric potential V at any point x, y, z (all in metres) in space is given by V = 4x² volts. The electric field (in V/m) at the point (1 m, 0, 2 m) is
questions.explanation: E = -dV/dx î = -8x î. At x=1: E = -8 î V/m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In the network shown below, the equivalent resistance between A and B is (Wheatstone bridge configuration with 5 resistors R)
questions.explanation: In a balanced Wheatstone bridge, the middle resistor carries no current. The equivalent is (2R×2R)/(2R+2R) = R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A long solenoid of N turns has a self-inductance L and area of cross-section A. When a current i flows through the solenoid, the magnetic field inside it has magnitude B. The current i is equal to
questions.explanation: B = μ₀ni = μ₀(N/l)i. L = μ₀N²A/l. So μ₀N/l = L/(NA). B = Li/(NA). i = BNA/L = BAN/L.

questions.text: If a potential difference of 20,000 volts is applied across an X-ray tube, the cut-off wavelength will be

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The count rate from 100 cm³ of a radioactive liquid is r. Some of this liquid is now discarded. The count rate of the remaining liquid is found to be r/10 after three half-lives. The volume of the remaining liquid, in cm³, is
questions.explanation: After 3 half-lives, activity drops by factor 1/8. If volume V remains: count rate = r×(V/100)×(1/8) = r/10. V/800 = 1/10. V = 80 cm³.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a Coolidge tube, the potential difference across the tube is 20 kV; and 10 mA current flows through the supply. Only 0.5% of the energy carried by the electrons striking the target is converted into X-rays. The X-ray beam carries a power of
questions.explanation: Power input = VI = 20000 × 0.01 = 200 W. X-ray power = 0.5% × 200 = 1 W.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a hydrogen atom emits a photon in going from n=5 to n=1, its recoil speed is almost

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: A compound contains 54.55% carbon, 9.09% hydrogen, 36.36% oxygen. The empirical formula of this compound is

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The dissociation constant of two acids HA₁ and HA₂ are 3.14 × 10⁻⁴ and 1.96 × 10⁻⁵ respectively. The relative strength of the acids will be approximately

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Oxidation number of H in NaH, CaH₂ and LiH, respectively is
questions.explanation: In metal hydrides (NaH, CaH₂, LiH), hydrogen is in -1 oxidation state.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: If 0.5 A current is passed through acidified silver nitrate solution for 100 minutes, the mass of silver deposited on cathode is (eq. wt. of silver nitrate = 108)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The metals A and B form oxide but B also forms nitride when both burn in air. The A and B are
questions.explanation: Mg burns in air to form both MgO (oxide) and Mg₃N₂ (nitride). K forms only oxide/peroxide. So A=K (oxide only) and B=Mg (oxide + nitride).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Benzene → (HNO₃/H₂SO₄) → A → (Br₂/FeBr₂) → B. The compound B is
questions.optionA: m-bromonitrobenzene (NO₂ and Br on same side)
questions.explanation: Benzene + HNO₃/H₂SO₄ → nitrobenzene (A). NO₂ is meta-directing, so Br₂/FeBr₂ gives m-bromonitrobenzene.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: The reaction conditions leading to the best yields of C₆H₅Cl are:
questions.explanation: C₆H₅Cl (chlorobenzene) is formed by electrophilic substitution. But wait, UV light gives free radical substitution on benzene (addition). For C₆H₅Cl via substitution, need Cl₂/FeCl₃. With UV, Cl₂ + benzene → C₆H₆Cl₆ (addition). So the question likely refers to chlorination in light conditions. With excess benzene and Cl₂ in UV light, mono-substitution is favored.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Mark the correct increasing order of reactivity of the following compounds with HBr/HCl: (A) benzyl alcohol with NO₂ group, (B) benzyl alcohol, (C) benzyl alcohol with Cl group
questions.explanation: NO₂ is electron-withdrawing (deactivates), Cl is weakly deactivating. Reactivity with HBr: electron-donating groups increase reactivity via stabilizing the carbocation. Order: NO₂ < unsubstituted < Cl. A < B < C.

questions.subject: English
questions.passage: I believe that only one person in a thousand knows the trick of really living in the present. Most of us spend 59 minutes an hour living in the past, with regret for lost joys, or shame for things badly done (both utterly useless and weakening) – or in a future which we either long for or dread. Yet the past is gone beyond repair, and every minute you spend in the vain effort to anticipate the future is a moment lost. There is only one world, the world pressing against you at this minute. There is only one minute in which you are alive, this miracle – here and now. The only way to live is by accepting every minute as an unrepeatable miracle. Which is exactly what it is – a miracle and unrepeatable.
questions.text: People live either in the past or in the future
questions.optionA: because they are in the habit of chewing the cud
questions.optionB: because they are a prey to imaginary fears
questions.optionC: because they are chained to memory and desire
questions.explanation: The passage explains people live in the past (regret/shame = memory) or future (longing/dread = desire). They are chained to memory and desire.

questions.subject: English
questions.text: What is an unrepeatable miracle?
questions.optionA: Every moment that we live and spend
questions.optionC: To crave for what is not
questions.explanation: The passage states: 'The only way to live is by accepting every minute as an unrepeatable miracle.'

questions.subject: English
questions.text: The attitude of the author is
questions.explanation: The author's attitude is pragmatic – focusing on practical acceptance of the present moment rather than dwelling on past or future.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimensional formula of Planck's constant is the same as that of
questions.explanation: Planck's constant h has dimensions [ML²T⁻¹], same as angular momentum (L = mvr → [ML²T⁻¹]).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the percentage error in the measurement of radius of a sphere is 2%, then the percentage error in its volume is
questions.explanation: V = (4/3)πr³. Percentage error in V = 3 × (% error in r) = 3 × 2% = 6%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two vectors of magnitudes 3 and 4 are perpendicular to each other. The magnitude of their resultant is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is thrown vertically upward with velocity u. The maximum height reached is
questions.explanation: Using v² = u² − 2gh, at max height v = 0, so h = u²/(2g).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A projectile is fired at 30° to the horizontal with velocity 20 m/s. Its horizontal range is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The time of flight of a projectile on a horizontal plane is
questions.explanation: Time of flight T = 2u sinθ/g, derived from the vertical component of motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A stone is dropped from a height of 45 m. How long does it take to reach the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: h = ½gt² → 45 = ½×10×t² → t² = 9 → t = 3 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The angle between two vectors A and B is θ. The magnitude of their cross product is
questions.explanation: |A × B| = AB sinθ, by definition of cross product.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Which of the following is a dimensionless quantity?
questions.explanation: Strain = ΔL/L, ratio of same quantities, hence dimensionless.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A boat crosses a river of width d with velocity v relative to water while the river flows with velocity u. The drift along the river is
questions.explanation: Time to cross = d/v. Drift = u × (d/v) = ud/v.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimensional formula of gravitational constant G is
questions.explanation: From F = Gm₁m₂/r², G = Fr²/(m₁m₂), so [G] = [MLT⁻²][L²]/[M²] = [M⁻¹L³T⁻²].

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two projectiles are fired at angles θ and (90°−θ) with the same speed. Their ranges are
questions.explanation: R = u²sin2θ/g. sin2(90°−θ) = sin(180°−2θ) = sin2θ. So ranges are equal.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If two forces of 5 N and 12 N act on a body, the maximum resultant is
questions.explanation: Maximum resultant = 5 + 12 = 17 N (when acting in the same direction).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball is thrown upward with 20 m/s. After how much time will it return to the thrower? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Total time = 2u/g = 2×20/10 = 4 s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The unit vector along 3î + 4ĵ is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Newton's first law of motion defines
questions.explanation: Newton's first law is also called the law of inertia. It defines inertia as the tendency of a body to resist change in its state of rest or uniform motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 5 kg body is acted upon by a net force of 20 N. Its acceleration is
questions.explanation: F = ma → a = F/m = 20/5 = 4 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A gun of mass M fires a bullet of mass m with velocity v. The recoil velocity of the gun is
questions.explanation: By conservation of momentum: 0 = mv − MV → V = mv/M.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg is on a rough surface with coefficient of friction 0.5. The force of friction is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A lift accelerates upward with acceleration a. The apparent weight of a person of mass m in the lift is
questions.explanation: When lift accelerates upward, N − mg = ma → N = m(g+a).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The impulse of a force is equal to the change in
questions.explanation: Impulse = F×t = Δp (change in momentum), from Newton's second law.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The coefficient of static friction is always
questions.optionB: greater than or equal to kinetic friction
questions.explanation: Static friction coefficient μₛ ≥ μₖ (kinetic friction coefficient). It takes more force to start motion than to maintain it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block of mass 10 kg is pushed along a frictionless surface by a force of 50 N for 4 s starting from rest. The final velocity is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two bodies of masses m and 2m are connected by a string over a frictionless pulley. The acceleration of the system is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Angle of friction is the angle between
questions.optionC: resultant of friction and normal reaction, and the normal reaction
questions.explanation: Angle of friction (λ) is the angle between the resultant contact force (R) and the normal reaction (N), where tan λ = μ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Work done by a force is zero when the angle between force and displacement is
questions.explanation: W = Fd cosθ. When θ = 90°, cos90° = 0, so W = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the momentum of a body is doubled, its kinetic energy becomes
questions.explanation: KE = p²/(2m). If p → 2p, KE → (2p)²/(2m) = 4p²/(2m) = 4 × original KE.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A spring of force constant k is stretched by x. The elastic potential energy stored is
questions.explanation: Elastic PE = ½kx², work done in stretching a spring.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a perfectly inelastic collision, which quantity is conserved?
questions.explanation: In perfectly inelastic collision, momentum is conserved but kinetic energy is not (some is converted to heat/deformation).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg moving with 4 m/s collides with a stationary body of same mass. If collision is perfectly elastic, the velocity of the first body after collision is
questions.explanation: In elastic collision between equal masses, the first body stops and the second moves with the velocity of the first.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Instantaneous power is defined as
questions.explanation: P = dW/dt = F·ds/dt = F·v. Power equals force dot velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work-energy theorem states that
questions.optionA: work done equals change in potential energy
questions.optionB: work done by net force equals change in kinetic energy
questions.optionC: work done equals force times time
questions.optionD: work done equals change in momentum
questions.explanation: The work-energy theorem: W_net = ΔKE = ½mv² − ½mu².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Gravitational force is a
questions.explanation: Gravitational force is conservative because work done by it depends only on initial and final positions, not on the path.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 5 kg is raised to a height of 10 m. The potential energy gained is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The centripetal acceleration of a body moving in a circle of radius r with speed v is
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration a = v²/r, directed toward the center.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a conical pendulum of string length L and half angle θ, the time period is
questions.explanation: For conical pendulum: T = 2π√(Lcosθ/g), derived from balancing tension components.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The banking angle θ of a road for a vehicle moving with speed v on a curve of radius r is given by
questions.explanation: For banking without friction: tanθ = v²/(rg).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The escape velocity from the surface of the Earth is approximately
questions.explanation: Escape velocity = √(2gR) ≈ √(2 × 9.8 × 6.4×10⁶) ≈ 11.2 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The value of acceleration due to gravity at the centre of the Earth is
questions.explanation: g at depth d: g' = g(1 − d/R). At center d = R, so g' = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The orbital velocity of a satellite close to Earth's surface is
questions.explanation: Orbital velocity v₀ = √(gR) ≈ √(9.8 × 6.4×10⁶) ≈ 7.9 km/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The relation between escape velocity (vₑ) and orbital velocity (v₀) is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The time period of a simple pendulum of length L is
questions.explanation: T = 2π√(L/g) for a simple pendulum with small oscillations.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In SHM, the acceleration is maximum at
questions.explanation: a = −ω²x. Maximum when x = A (amplitude), i.e., at extreme position.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The total energy of a particle in SHM is proportional to
questions.explanation: E = ½mω²A². Total energy ∝ A².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The time period of a spring-mass system is
questions.explanation: T = 2π√(m/k) for a spring-mass system.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: At the mean position in SHM, the velocity is
questions.explanation: v = ω√(A² − x²). At mean position x = 0, v = ωA = maximum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The gravitational potential energy of a mass m at distance r from center of Earth (r > R) is
questions.explanation: Gravitational PE = −GMm/r for a point mass at distance r from center of Earth.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The moment of inertia of a solid sphere about its diameter is
questions.explanation: I = ⅖MR² for a solid sphere about its diameter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The radius of gyration of a body is
questions.explanation: I = Mk² → k = √(I/M), where k is the radius of gyration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Angular momentum of a body is conserved when
questions.explanation: τ = dL/dt. When net torque τ = 0, angular momentum L is conserved.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The rotational kinetic energy of a body is
questions.explanation: Rotational KE = ½Iω², analogous to translational KE = ½mv².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Torque is the cross product of
questions.explanation: τ = r × F, the cross product of position vector and force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The moment of inertia of a thin ring about an axis through its centre perpendicular to its plane is
questions.explanation: For a thin ring, all mass is at distance R from axis: I = MR².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: According to the parallel axis theorem, I =
questions.explanation: Parallel axis theorem: I = I_cm + Md², where d is the distance between the axes.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Hooke's law is valid up to the
questions.explanation: Hooke's law (stress ∝ strain) is valid only up to the proportional limit.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Young's modulus is the ratio of
questions.explanation: Y = Normal stress / Longitudinal strain = (F/A)/(ΔL/L).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Bulk modulus is defined as
questions.explanation: Bulk modulus K = Volume stress / Volume strain = −ΔP/(ΔV/V).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Poisson's ratio is the ratio of
questions.explanation: Poisson's ratio σ = Lateral strain / Longitudinal strain.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The elastic energy stored per unit volume in a stretched wire is
questions.explanation: Energy per unit volume = ½ × stress × strain = ½ × Y × (strain)².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The modulus of rigidity relates to
questions.explanation: Modulus of rigidity (shear modulus) = shear stress/shear strain, applies to shape changes without volume change.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Archimedes' principle states that the buoyant force on a submerged body equals
questions.explanation: Buoyant force = weight of the fluid displaced by the body.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body floats when its density is
questions.optionB: equal to or less than fluid density
questions.optionC: always exactly equal to fluid density
questions.explanation: A body floats when ρ_body ≤ ρ_fluid.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Surface tension has the SI unit of
questions.explanation: Surface tension = Force/Length, SI unit is N/m.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The rise of liquid in a capillary tube of radius r is h =
questions.explanation: Capillary rise: h = 2T cosθ/(ρgr), where T is surface tension, θ contact angle, r tube radius.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The terminal velocity of a sphere falling through a viscous fluid is proportional to
questions.explanation: From Stoke's law: v_t = 2r²(ρ−σ)g/(9η). Terminal velocity ∝ r².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Bernoulli's theorem is a statement of conservation of
questions.explanation: Bernoulli's equation: P + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant, represents conservation of energy per unit volume.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The equation of continuity for incompressible fluid flow is
questions.explanation: Equation of continuity: A₁v₁ = A₂v₂ (for incompressible fluid).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Reynolds number determines
questions.explanation: Reynolds number Re = ρvD/η. If Re < 2000, flow is laminar; Re > 4000, turbulent.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Viscosity of a liquid decreases with increase in
questions.explanation: Viscosity of liquids decreases with temperature as intermolecular forces weaken.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The SI unit of dynamic viscosity is
questions.explanation: SI unit of dynamic viscosity is Pascal-second (Pa·s) or N·s/m².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Poiseuille's formula gives the volume rate of flow Q =
questions.explanation: Q = πPr⁴/(8ηL), where P is pressure difference, r radius, η viscosity, L length of tube.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The angle of contact for mercury-glass interface is
questions.explanation: Mercury does not wet glass, so the angle of contact is obtuse (>90°), causing depression in capillary.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Excess pressure inside a soap bubble of radius r and surface tension T is
questions.explanation: Soap bubble has two free surfaces, so excess pressure = 4T/r (for a liquid drop it's 2T/r).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body projected at 45° achieves maximum
questions.explanation: R = u²sin2θ/g is maximum when 2θ = 90°, i.e., θ = 45°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimension of pressure is
questions.explanation: Pressure = Force/Area = [MLT⁻²]/[L²] = [ML⁻¹T⁻²].

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the length of a simple pendulum is increased by 4 times, the time period

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The acceleration due to gravity at height h above the Earth's surface is (h << R)
questions.explanation: For h << R: g' = g(1 − 2h/R), using binomial approximation of g' = gR²/(R+h)².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The time period of a geostationary satellite is
questions.explanation: A geostationary satellite orbits with the same period as Earth's rotation: 24 hours.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In damped oscillation, the amplitude
questions.explanation: In damped oscillation, A = A₀e^(−bt/2m), amplitude decreases exponentially.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Resonance occurs in forced oscillation when
questions.explanation: Resonance occurs when the driving frequency matches the natural frequency of the system.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two springs of spring constants k₁ and k₂ connected in series have effective spring constant
questions.explanation: In series: 1/k_eff = 1/k₁ + 1/k₂ → k_eff = k₁k₂/(k₁+k₂).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two springs of spring constants k₁ and k₂ connected in parallel have effective spring constant
questions.explanation: In parallel: k_eff = k₁ + k₂.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Kepler's third law states that T² is proportional to
questions.explanation: Kepler's third law: T² ∝ r³, where r is the semi-major axis of the orbit.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 10 kg moves with velocity 3 m/s. Its kinetic energy is
questions.explanation: KE = ½mv² = ½ × 10 × 9 = 45 J.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The centre of mass of a uniform triangular lamina lies at its
questions.explanation: For a uniform triangular lamina, the center of mass is at the centroid (intersection of medians).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A disc and a ring of same mass and radius roll down an incline without slipping. Which reaches the bottom first?
questions.explanation: Disc has smaller I (½MR²) than ring (MR²), so disc has larger acceleration a = gsinθ/(1+I/MR²) and reaches first.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The moment of inertia of a solid disc about an axis through its centre perpendicular to its plane is
questions.explanation: I = ½MR² for a solid disc about the perpendicular axis through its centre.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An ice skater spinning with arms outstretched pulls arms in. What happens to angular velocity?
questions.explanation: L = Iω is conserved. When I decreases (arms pulled in), ω must increase.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The velocity of efflux from an orifice at depth h below the surface of a liquid is
questions.explanation: Torricelli's theorem: v = √(2gh), derived from Bernoulli's equation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body weighs 50 N in air and 40 N in water. The buoyant force is
questions.explanation: Buoyant force = Weight in air − Weight in water = 50 − 40 = 10 N.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The equation v = u + at represents motion with
questions.explanation: v = u + at is valid for uniformly accelerated motion (constant a).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The area under a velocity-time graph gives
questions.explanation: Displacement = ∫v dt = area under v-t graph.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The slope of a displacement-time graph gives
questions.explanation: v = ds/dt, so the slope of s-t graph gives velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wire of length L and radius r is stretched by a force F. If the radius is doubled, the elongation becomes
questions.explanation: ΔL = FL/(AY) = FL/(πr²Y). If r → 2r, ΔL → FL/(4πr²Y) = ΔL/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The value of g at a depth d below the Earth's surface is
questions.explanation: At depth d: g' = g(1 − d/R).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Rocket propulsion works on the principle of
questions.explanation: Rockets expel gas backward (action), gaining forward thrust (reaction) — Newton's third law.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The maximum height reached by a projectile fired at angle θ with velocity u is
questions.explanation: H = u²sin²θ/(2g), from vertical motion: 0 = (usinθ)² − 2gH.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Stoke's law gives the viscous drag force on a sphere as
questions.explanation: Stoke's law: F = 6πηrv, where η is viscosity, r is radius, v is velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The acceleration of a body sliding down a smooth inclined plane of angle θ is
questions.explanation: On a smooth incline, the component of g along the plane is g sinθ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A satellite revolves around the Earth. If its orbital radius is doubled, its orbital velocity becomes
questions.explanation: v₀ = √(GM/r). If r → 2r, v₀ → √(GM/2r) = v₀/√2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimensional formula of angular velocity is
questions.explanation: ω = θ/t, where θ is dimensionless (radians). So [ω] = [T⁻¹] = [M⁰L⁰T⁻¹].

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle is projected with velocity u at angle θ. The velocity at the highest point is
questions.explanation: At the highest point, vertical component = 0. Only horizontal component u cosθ remains.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the kinetic energy of a body is doubled, the momentum becomes
questions.explanation: KE = p²/(2m). If KE → 2KE, then p² → 2p², so p → √2 p.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 1 kW engine can lift a mass of 200 kg to a height of (g = 10 m/s²) in 4 s

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The minimum number of unequal vectors whose resultant can be zero is
questions.explanation: Two unequal vectors can't give zero resultant. Three unequal coplanar vectors can form a closed triangle, giving zero resultant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass m is moving in a circle of radius r with constant speed v. The centripetal force is
questions.explanation: Centripetal force F = mv²/r = mω²r.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball of mass 0.1 kg is thrown against a wall with velocity 10 m/s and bounces back with 8 m/s. The impulse is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The displacement of a body in the nth second of its motion is given by
questions.explanation: s_n = u + a(2n−1)/2, displacement in the nth second for uniform acceleration.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is dropped from a tower. The ratio of distances fallen in the 1st, 2nd, and 3rd seconds is
questions.explanation: Distances in successive seconds from rest are in ratio 1:3:5:7... (odd number ratio).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The coefficient of restitution for a perfectly elastic collision is
questions.explanation: e = (v₂−v₁)/(u₁−u₂). For perfectly elastic collision, e = 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The coefficient of restitution for a perfectly inelastic collision is
questions.explanation: For perfectly inelastic collision, bodies stick together and e = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimensional formula of impulse is the same as that of
questions.explanation: Impulse = FΔt = [MLT⁻²][T] = [MLT⁻¹] = dimension of momentum.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A block rests on a rough incline of angle θ. The minimum coefficient of friction for equilibrium is
questions.explanation: For equilibrium: μmg cosθ ≥ mg sinθ → μ ≥ tanθ. Minimum μ = tanθ.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a man walks on a surface, the force that propels him forward is
questions.explanation: Static friction between feet and ground pushes the person forward (reaction to backward push of feet).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A vehicle moves on a banked curve of radius r and banking angle θ at speed v = √(rg tanθ). The friction force needed is
questions.explanation: At ideal speed v = √(rg tanθ), the horizontal component of normal reaction provides centripetal force. No friction needed.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A stone tied to a string is whirled in a vertical circle. At the top, the minimum speed to maintain circular motion is
questions.explanation: At the top, mg = mv²/r (when tension = 0) → v = √(rg).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The minimum speed at the bottom for a stone to complete a vertical circle of radius r is
questions.explanation: Using energy conservation: ½mv²_bottom = ½mv²_top + mg(2r). With v_top = √(rg), v_bottom = √(5rg).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The gravitational field intensity at a point is defined as
questions.explanation: Gravitational field intensity E = F/m = GM/r², it is force per unit test mass.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The total energy of a satellite in orbit is
questions.explanation: E = KE + PE = GMm/(2r) − GMm/r = −GMm/(2r) < 0. Bound system has negative total energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In SHM y = A sinωt, the phase of the particle at t = T/4 is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The frequency of a seconds pendulum is
questions.explanation: A seconds pendulum has T = 2 s, so f = 1/T = 0.5 Hz.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the mass attached to a spring is increased 4 times, the time period becomes

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The perpendicular axis theorem is applicable to
questions.explanation: Perpendicular axis theorem I_z = I_x + I_y applies only to planar laminae.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid cylinder rolls down an incline without slipping. Its acceleration is
questions.explanation: a = g sinθ/(1 + I/MR²) = g sinθ/(1 + ½) = ⅔ g sinθ for a solid cylinder (I = ½MR²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimensional formula of torque is
questions.explanation: τ = r × F → [L][MLT⁻²] = [ML²T⁻²], same as energy but different physical quantity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The moment of inertia of a hollow sphere about its diameter is
questions.explanation: I = ⅔MR² for a thin hollow sphere (spherical shell) about its diameter.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wire is stretched to double its length. Its Young's modulus
questions.explanation: Young's modulus is a material property and does not change with dimensions of the wire.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The breaking stress of a wire depends on
questions.explanation: Breaking stress is a material property; it does not depend on dimensions of the wire.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The relation between Y, K and η (Young's modulus, Bulk modulus and Rigidity modulus) is
questions.explanation: The relation is Y = 9Kη/(3K+η), connecting all three elastic moduli.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Excess pressure inside a liquid drop of radius r and surface tension T is
questions.explanation: For a liquid drop (single surface): ΔP = 2T/r.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wooden block floats in water with ⅔ of its volume submerged. Its density is
questions.explanation: ρ_block/ρ_water = V_submerged/V_total = 2/3. ρ_block = (2/3)×1000 = 667 kg/m³.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In streamline flow, the rate of flow of liquid through a tube is directly proportional to
questions.explanation: By Poiseuille's formula, Q = πPr⁴/(8ηL). Rate of flow ∝ r⁴.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The energy stored in a stretched wire per unit volume is
questions.explanation: Energy/volume = ½ × stress × strain = ½ × Y × strain² (since stress = Y × strain).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car of mass 1000 kg negotiates a banked curve of radius 100 m at 10 m/s. The banking angle is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The dimensional formula of surface tension is
questions.explanation: Surface tension = Force/length = [MLT⁻²]/[L] = [MT⁻²].

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two bodies A and B of masses m and 4m are dropped from the same height. The ratio of their velocities on reaching the ground is
questions.explanation: v = √(2gh), independent of mass. Both reach with same velocity. Ratio = 1:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is projected with velocity u at angle θ. The radius of curvature at the highest point is
questions.explanation: At highest point, v = ucosθ. Centripetal acceleration = g. So r = v²/g = u²cos²θ/g.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An object is placed in a lift which is falling freely. The weight of the object is
questions.explanation: In free fall, a = g. Apparent weight = m(g−a) = m(g−g) = 0 (weightlessness).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A horse has to pull harder during the start of the motion than later because
questions.optionA: kinetic friction is more than static friction
questions.optionB: static friction is more than kinetic friction
questions.explanation: Static friction (before motion) > kinetic friction (during motion). More force needed initially.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The loss in kinetic energy in a perfectly inelastic collision between two equal masses, one at rest, is
questions.explanation: KE_initial = ½mu². After collision v = u/2 (momentum conservation). KE_final = ½(2m)(u/2)² = ¼mu². Loss = 50%.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The velocity of a body in SHM at displacement x from mean position is
questions.explanation: In SHM: v = ω√(A²−x²).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A planet moves faster when it is closer to the Sun. This is explained by
questions.explanation: Kepler's second law (law of areas): The line joining the planet and Sun sweeps equal areas in equal times. Closer → faster.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The escape velocity is independent of
questions.explanation: v_e = √(2GM/R). It depends on M and R of the planet but not on the mass of the body.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The weight of a body at the poles is
questions.explanation: At equator, effective g is reduced by centrifugal effect: g_eff = g − ω²R. At poles, ω effect is zero. So weight is more at poles.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Dimensional analysis can be used to
questions.optionA: derive exact formulas with dimensionless constants
questions.optionB: check the correctness of a physical equation
questions.explanation: Dimensional analysis can check dimensional consistency of equations but cannot determine dimensionless constants or derive trigonometric relations.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body drops from height h and bounces to height h/4. The coefficient of restitution is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The angular momentum of a particle of mass m moving with velocity v in a circle of radius r is
questions.explanation: L = mvr = Iω for a particle in circular motion.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A solid cylinder and a hollow cylinder of same mass and radius roll down an incline. Which reaches first?
questions.explanation: Solid cylinder (I = ½MR²) has smaller moment of inertia ratio than hollow cylinder (I = MR²), so it accelerates faster.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The Poisson's ratio for a perfectly incompressible material is
questions.explanation: For incompressible material, volume doesn't change: σ = 0.5 (maximum value for isotropic material).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A steel wire and a rubber band have the same length and cross-section. Which has greater Young's modulus?
questions.explanation: Steel is much stiffer than rubber. For the same stress, steel deforms less, so Y_steel >> Y_rubber.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done by centripetal force on a body moving in a circle is
questions.explanation: Centripetal force is perpendicular to velocity. W = F·d·cos90° = 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg has momentum 10 kg·m/s. Its kinetic energy is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A force F = 3î + 4ĵ N acts on a body. The magnitude of the force is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A pendulum clock runs fast in winter because
questions.explanation: In winter, temperature drops → pendulum length contracts → T decreases → clock runs fast.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body starting from rest has displacement proportional to t². Its acceleration is
questions.explanation: s ∝ t² implies s = ct² = ½at². So a = 2c = constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moves in a straight line with velocity v = 3t² + 2t. The acceleration at t = 2 s is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The action and reaction forces in Newton's third law
questions.explanation: Action and reaction are equal, opposite, and act on different bodies, so they never cancel each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The friction force on a body sliding on a surface is independent of
questions.explanation: Friction f = μN is independent of the area of contact (for dry friction).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The time period of a simple pendulum on the moon (g_moon = g/6) compared to Earth is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A spring balance reads 10 kg when a bucket of water is hung. A block of 2 kg and density 4000 kg/m³ is immersed in water (attached separately). The balance reads
questions.explanation: Volume of block = 2/4000 = 5×10⁻⁴ m³. Reaction force on water = buoyant force = ρ_w × V × g = 1000 × 5×10⁻⁴ × g = 0.5g N. Balance reads 10 + 0.5 = 10.5 kg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The number of significant figures in 0.00340 is
questions.explanation: Leading zeros are not significant. 0.00340 has 3 significant figures: 3, 4, and the trailing 0.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Which pair has same dimensions?
questions.explanation: Work = F·d = [ML²T⁻²]. Torque = r × F = [ML²T⁻²]. Same dimensions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body falls freely from rest. The distance fallen in the 4th second is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A bullet of mass 50 g moving at 400 m/s penetrates 10 cm into a wall. The average resistive force is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 60 kg man stands in a lift moving upward with acceleration 2 m/s². The reading of a weighing scale is (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Apparent weight = m(g+a) = 60(10+2) = 720 N.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass m moving with velocity v collides with another body of mass 2m at rest and sticks to it. The common velocity after collision is
questions.explanation: By conservation of momentum: mv = (m+2m)V → V = v/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A ball is projected at 60° with horizontal at 20 m/s. The maximum height reached is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The tension in a string of length L whirling a mass m in a horizontal circle at speed v is
questions.explanation: Tension provides centripetal force: T = mv²/L (for horizontal circle, ignoring gravity).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The height of a geostationary orbit above the Earth's surface is approximately
questions.explanation: Geostationary orbit height ≈ 36,000 km above Earth's surface (orbital radius ≈ 42,000 km from center).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the frequency of SHM is doubled, the maximum acceleration becomes
questions.explanation: a_max = ω²A = (2πf)²A. If f → 2f, a_max → 4(2πf)²A = 4 × original.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The ratio of kinetic energy to potential energy of a particle in SHM at a displacement of A/2 is
questions.explanation: KE = ½mω²(A²−x²), PE = ½mω²x². At x = A/2: KE/PE = (A²−A²/4)/(A²/4) = (3A²/4)/(A²/4) = 3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A hollow and a solid sphere of same mass and radius are released from rest on an incline. Which reaches the bottom first?
questions.explanation: Solid sphere (I = ⅖MR²) has smaller I/MR² = 0.4 compared to hollow sphere (I = ⅔MR², ratio = 0.67). Greater acceleration for solid sphere.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The moment of inertia of a thin rod of mass M and length L about an axis through one end perpendicular to its length is
questions.explanation: I_end = I_cm + Md² = ML²/12 + M(L/2)² = ML²/12 + ML²/4 = ML²/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The moment of inertia of a thin rod about its centre and perpendicular to its length is
questions.explanation: I = ML²/12 for a thin rod about its centre.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wire of Young's modulus Y, length L and area A is loaded by weight mg. The extension produced is
questions.explanation: Y = (F/A)/(ΔL/L) → ΔL = FL/(YA) = mgL/(YA).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The Poisson's ratio of a material is always between
questions.explanation: Theoretically, for isotropic materials: −1 ≤ σ ≤ 0.5. Practically for most materials: 0 ≤ σ ≤ 0.5.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A hydraulic press works on the principle of
questions.explanation: Hydraulic press uses Pascal's law: pressure applied at one point is transmitted undiminished throughout the fluid.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The meniscus of water in a glass tube is
questions.explanation: Water wets glass (contact angle < 90°), so the meniscus is concave (curves upward at edges).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: An aeroplane wing generates lift due to
questions.explanation: The wing shape makes air flow faster above → lower pressure above → net upward force (Bernoulli's principle).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Detergents clean clothes by reducing
questions.explanation: Detergents reduce surface tension of water, allowing it to penetrate fabric fibers and remove dirt.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The velocity-time graph of a body moving with constant velocity is a
questions.optionA: straight line parallel to time axis
questions.optionB: straight line parallel to velocity axis
questions.explanation: Constant velocity means v doesn't change with time, giving a horizontal line on v-t graph.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The position of a body is given by x = 2t³ − 5t² + 10. Its acceleration at t = 1 s is
questions.explanation: v = dx/dt = 6t² − 10t. a = dv/dt = 12t − 10. At t = 1: a = 12 − 10 = 2 m/s².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the resultant of two equal forces has the same magnitude as each force, the angle between them is
questions.explanation: R² = F² + F² + 2F²cosθ = F². So 2F²(1+cosθ) = F² → cosθ = −1/2 → θ = 120°.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The ratio of the ranges of two projectiles launched at 30° and 60° with the same speed is
questions.explanation: R = u²sin2θ/g. sin60° = sin120° = √3/2. Both give the same range. Ratio = 1:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A rope can withstand a tension of 800 N. The maximum mass it can lift with acceleration 2 m/s² is (g = 10 m/s²)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body travels first half distance with speed v₁ and second half with speed v₂. The average speed is
questions.explanation: For equal distances: v_avg = 2v₁v₂/(v₁+v₂) (harmonic mean).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The acceleration of a body rolling down a smooth incline of angle θ without slipping is gsinθ/(1+k²/R²). For a sphere, k²/R² =
questions.explanation: For solid sphere I = ⅖MR², so k² = I/M = ⅖R². Thus k²/R² = 2/5.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The total kinetic energy of a body rolling without slipping is
questions.explanation: Total KE = translational KE + rotational KE = ½mv² + ½Iω².

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wire has a breaking force F. If the wire is doubled in thickness, the breaking force becomes
questions.explanation: Breaking force = breaking stress × area. If thickness (diameter) doubles, area quadruples. Force = 4F.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The pressure at a depth h in a fluid of density ρ is
questions.explanation: Gauge pressure at depth h: P = ρgh.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When water flows through a constriction in a pipe, its velocity
questions.explanation: By equation of continuity A₁v₁ = A₂v₂. Smaller area → larger velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The wetting property of a liquid depends on
questions.explanation: Wetting depends on the contact angle, which is determined by relative magnitudes of cohesive and adhesive forces (related to surface tension).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A satellite in orbit experiences
questions.optionA: weightlessness because there is no gravity
questions.optionB: weightlessness because gravitational force provides centripetal force
questions.explanation: Satellite and contents are in free fall (gravity = centripetal force). No normal reaction → apparent weightlessness.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body performs SHM with period T. The time taken to travel from x = 0 to x = A/2 is
questions.explanation: x = Asinωt. A/2 = Asinωt → sinωt = 1/2 → ωt = π/6 → t = T/12.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The frequency of oscillation of a simple pendulum does not depend on
questions.explanation: T = 2π√(L/g). Frequency depends on L and g but not on mass of the bob.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The thrust on a rocket is given by
questions.explanation: Rocket thrust F = v_rel × (dm/dt), where v_rel is the exhaust velocity and dm/dt is the rate of mass ejection.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The force constant of a spring is 200 N/m. A 2 kg mass attached to it oscillates. The time period is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A Venturi meter works on the principle of
questions.explanation: Venturi meter measures flow rate using Bernoulli's principle: higher velocity at constriction → lower pressure.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body moving with initial velocity u decelerates uniformly at rate a and comes to rest. The distance traveled is
questions.explanation: v² = u² − 2as. At rest v = 0: s = u²/(2a).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a particle undergoing SHM, the graph of acceleration vs displacement is
questions.optionB: a straight line with negative slope
questions.explanation: a = −ω²x. The graph of a vs x is a straight line through origin with slope −ω² (negative).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The SI unit of specific heat capacity is
questions.explanation: Specific heat c = Q/(mΔT), so SI unit is J/(kg·K).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The zeroth law of thermodynamics defines
questions.explanation: The zeroth law defines the concept of temperature through thermal equilibrium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The triple point of water is
questions.explanation: Triple point of water = 273.16 K = 0.01°C, where solid, liquid, and vapor coexist.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Newton's law of cooling states that rate of cooling is proportional to
questions.optionC: difference in temperature between body and surroundings
questions.explanation: dT/dt ∝ (T − T₀), where T₀ is the temperature of surroundings.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The latent heat of fusion of ice is approximately
questions.explanation: Latent heat of fusion of ice ≈ 80 cal/g = 336 kJ/kg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The latent heat of vaporization of water is approximately
questions.explanation: Latent heat of vaporization of water ≈ 540 cal/g = 2260 kJ/kg.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In the method of mixtures, heat lost by hot body equals
questions.explanation: By conservation of energy (no heat loss to surroundings): Heat lost = Heat gained.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The coefficient of linear expansion has the unit
questions.explanation: α = ΔL/(L₀ΔT). Unit = 1/K = K⁻¹.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The relation between coefficient of linear expansion (α) and coefficient of volume expansion (γ) is
questions.explanation: For isotropic solids: γ = 3α (volume expansion coefficient = 3 × linear expansion coefficient).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Water has maximum density at
questions.explanation: Anomalous expansion of water: water has maximum density at 4°C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The thermal conductivity of a material is measured in
questions.explanation: From Q/t = KA(ΔT)/L, K has unit W/(m·K).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A perfect black body
questions.optionB: absorbs all radiation falling on it
questions.explanation: A perfect black body absorbs all incident radiation regardless of wavelength.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Stefan-Boltzmann law states that total energy radiated per unit area is proportional to
questions.explanation: E = σT⁴. Total radiated energy per unit area ∝ T⁴.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The rate of heat conduction through a rod is given by Q/t =
questions.explanation: Fourier's law: Q/t = KA(T₁−T₂)/L, where K is thermal conductivity, A area, L length.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Convection is the mode of heat transfer in
questions.explanation: Convection involves actual movement of heated fluid particles and occurs only in fluids (liquids and gases).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The mean kinetic energy of a gas molecule at temperature T is
questions.explanation: Mean KE per molecule = ³⁄₂kT, from kinetic theory (½kT per degree of freedom × 3).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The rms speed of gas molecules is proportional to
questions.explanation: v_rms = √(3kT/m). So v_rms ∝ √T.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The first law of thermodynamics is a statement of conservation of
questions.explanation: First law: ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW. It is the conservation of energy applied to thermodynamic systems.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In an adiabatic process
questions.explanation: Adiabatic: ΔQ = 0. No heat exchange with surroundings.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In an isothermal process
questions.explanation: Isothermal: constant temperature. PV = nRT = constant → PV = constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For an ideal gas, the relation between Cp and Cv is
questions.explanation: Mayer's relation: Cp − Cv = R (per mole).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The efficiency of a Carnot engine working between temperatures T₁ and T₂ (T₁ > T₂) is
questions.explanation: η = 1 − T₂/T₁ (temperatures in Kelvin). This is the maximum possible efficiency.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The second law of thermodynamics implies that
questions.optionB: heat flows from hot to cold spontaneously
questions.optionD: work can be fully converted to heat
questions.explanation: Second law (Clausius statement): Heat cannot spontaneously flow from cold to hot body.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Entropy of the universe in any natural process
questions.explanation: For irreversible (natural) processes, ΔS_universe > 0. Entropy of the universe always increases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Work done in an isobaric process is
questions.explanation: At constant pressure: W = PΔV = P(V₂ − V₁).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Work done in an isochoric process is
questions.explanation: At constant volume: W = PΔV = 0 (since ΔV = 0).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a monoatomic ideal gas, Cv =
questions.explanation: Monoatomic gas has 3 degrees of freedom. Cv = (f/2)R = ³⁄₂R.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a diatomic gas at moderate temperatures, γ = Cp/Cv =
questions.explanation: Diatomic gas: f = 5 (3 translational + 2 rotational). γ = (f+2)/f = 7/5 = 1.4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The coefficient of performance (COP) of a refrigerator is
questions.explanation: COP = Q₂/W = Heat extracted from cold reservoir / Work input.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A Carnot engine has efficiency 40%. If the source temperature is 500 K, the sink temperature is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In an adiabatic process, PVᵞ =
questions.explanation: For adiabatic process: PVᵞ = constant, where γ = Cp/Cv.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The ideal gas equation is
questions.explanation: PV = nRT, where n = number of moles, R = universal gas constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Heat capacity at constant pressure is greater than at constant volume because
questions.optionA: work is done at constant pressure
questions.optionB: temperature rises more at constant P
questions.optionD: internal energy decreases at constant P
questions.explanation: At constant P, heat goes to both increasing internal energy AND doing expansion work. So Cp > Cv.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Wien's displacement law states that λ_max × T =
questions.explanation: λ_max T = b (Wien's constant ≈ 2.898 × 10⁻³ m·K). Higher T → shorter λ_max.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The rms speed of hydrogen molecules at 27°C is approximately (M = 2 g/mol, R = 8.3 J/mol·K)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two identical rods of different materials with thermal conductivities K₁ and K₂ are connected in series. The effective thermal conductivity is
questions.explanation: In series (same area, same length): 2L/(K_eff A) = L/(K₁A) + L/(K₂A) → K_eff = 2K₁K₂/(K₁+K₂).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Otto cycle is used in
questions.explanation: Otto cycle is the theoretical cycle for spark ignition (petrol/gasoline) engines.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Diesel cycle differs from Otto cycle in having
questions.optionA: isobaric heat addition instead of isochoric
questions.explanation: In Diesel cycle, heat addition is at constant pressure (isobaric), while in Otto cycle it's at constant volume (isochoric).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The efficiency of Otto cycle depends on
questions.explanation: η_Otto = 1 − 1/r^(γ−1), where r is the compression ratio.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Radiation does not require
questions.explanation: Radiation can travel through vacuum; it doesn't require any material medium.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: 10 g of ice at 0°C is mixed with 10 g of water at 80°C. The final temperature is (L = 80 cal/g)
questions.explanation: Heat needed to melt ice = 10×80 = 800 cal. Heat available from water = 10×1×80 = 800 cal. All heat is used to melt ice. Final temp = 0°C.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A metal rod is heated. Its length increases but density
questions.explanation: Mass remains same. Volume increases on heating. So density = m/V decreases.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A bimetallic strip bends on heating because
questions.optionB: the two metals have different coefficients of expansion
questions.explanation: Different coefficients of linear expansion cause unequal expansion, leading to bending.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The mean free path of gas molecules increases with
questions.explanation: λ = kT/(√2πd²P). Mean free path ∝ 1/P. Decreasing pressure increases mean free path.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The internal energy of an ideal gas depends only on
questions.explanation: For an ideal gas, U = nCvT. Internal energy depends only on temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a free expansion of an ideal gas
questions.explanation: Free expansion: W = 0, Q = 0 → ΔU = 0 → ΔT = 0 for ideal gas.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The change in entropy in a reversible isothermal process is
questions.explanation: ΔS = Q/T for a reversible isothermal process.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The work done by an ideal gas in an isothermal expansion from V₁ to V₂ is
questions.explanation: W = ∫PdV = ∫(nRT/V)dV = nRT ln(V₂/V₁) for isothermal process.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A Carnot engine operates between 600 K and 300 K. Its efficiency is

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Specific heat of a gas is
questions.optionC: depends on the process (Cp or Cv)
questions.explanation: Unlike solids/liquids, a gas has two specific heats: Cp (constant pressure) and Cv (constant volume).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: For a monoatomic gas, γ = Cp/Cv =
questions.explanation: Monoatomic: f = 3, γ = (f+2)/f = 5/3 ≈ 1.67.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The pressure exerted by an ideal gas is P = (1/3)ρv²_rms. Here ρ is
questions.explanation: P = (1/3)ρ⟨v²⟩ where ρ = nm = mass density of gas (mass per unit volume).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The slope of an adiabatic curve (P-V diagram) compared to an isothermal curve at the same point is
questions.explanation: Isothermal: dP/dV = −P/V. Adiabatic: dP/dV = −γP/V. Since γ > 1, adiabatic is steeper.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body at temperature T radiates energy proportional to
questions.explanation: Stefan-Boltzmann law: E = σT⁴. Energy radiated ∝ T⁴.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The degrees of freedom of a diatomic gas molecule at room temperature are
questions.explanation: Diatomic at room temperature: 3 translational + 2 rotational = 5 degrees of freedom.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Absolute zero temperature is theoretically the temperature at which
questions.explanation: At absolute zero (0 K = −273.15°C), molecular kinetic energy is minimum (classically zero).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The value of universal gas constant R is
questions.explanation: R = 8.314 J/(mol·K) is the universal gas constant.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The Dulong-Petit law states that the molar specific heat of solids at high temperature is approximately
questions.explanation: Dulong-Petit: Cv ≈ 3R ≈ 25 J/(mol·K) for solids at high temperatures.

questions.subject: Physics

questions.explanation: Charles' law: V ∝ T at constant pressure.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The heat capacity of water is high. This is important because

questions.subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The efficiency of a Carnot engine can be increased by
questions.explanation: η = 1 − T₂/T₁. Increasing T₁ or decreasing T₂ (or both) increases efficiency.

questions.subject: Physics
